prose:
Fukui reactivity analysis was performed on pyridine using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier standard) in the gas phase with charge 0, multiplicity 1, and PySCF as the computational backend. Mulliken partial charges were used to compute Fukui indices (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) by examining the neutral molecule and its singly charged cation and anion forms (both open-shell doublets, q = ±1, multiplicity 2). All three electronic states converged successfully.

The analysis identifies nitrogen (atom 1) as the most electrophilic site with f⁺ = 0.484, indicating high susceptibility to nucleophilic attack. The most nucleophilic site is carbon atom 4 with f⁻ = 0.118, showing modest selectivity for electrophilic attack. The dual descriptor (f⁺ − f⁻) shows strong selectivity at nitrogen (0.420) for nucleophilic addition, while meta/para carbons are relatively indiscriminate (f⁰ ≈ 0.022). Hydrogen atoms show negative electrophilicity and weak nucleophilicity, as expected. Fukui index charge conservation (∑f⁺ = ∑f⁻ = 1.0000) passed, confirming robust convergence. Visualization was saved as a PNG plot.</prose>
<parameter name="n_atoms">11
